vulnerabilities.description: Information Disclosure: Error message reveals internal supplement IDs (e.g., 'UI to grams conversion not configured for supplement: vitamina-d3'). This leaks the supplement database schema to clients. Recommendation: Use generic error messages and log details server-side only.

Incomplete PII Detection: The containsPII() function is imported but not shown in context. Without seeing its implementation, cannot verify if it catches all PII types (credit cards, phone numbers, SSN, email patterns, etc.). Risk: PII may leak into analytics storage despite filtering intent.

Temporary File Cleanup on Crash: While cleanup is in a finally block, process crash before finally executes could leave temp files in /tmp indefinitely. Though filenames use random hex and not predictable paths, this is still unclean. Recommendation: Implement OS-level temp file auto-cleanup or use a temp library that hooks process.on('exit').

Storage Quota Management: No visible quota enforcement when writing events to IndexedDB/localStorage. The #checkStorage() health check warns at 90%, but analytics data could fill quota and cause silent failures. No retention policy or data cleanup visible. Recommendation: Implement sliding window data retention (e.g., keep 30 days max) and warn/delete before quota is full.

Unclear Cache Versioning Strategy: Service Worker uses version tokens (CACHE_VERSION='v4.0.0') but no explicit max-age or revalidation strategy shown for dynamic cache entries. Stale data could persist indefinitely. Recommendation: Add explicit Cache-Control headers and revalidate-on-use logic for user data endpoints.

User Agent Redaction Completeness: redactUserAgent() is called on navigator.userAgent, but implementation not shown. User agents can still leak OS/browser versions. Recommendation: Replace with minimal format like 'Browser/OS' without version to reduce fingerprinting vector.

recommendations: Audit the containsPII() implementation immediately to verify it detects: email patterns, credit card numbers (Luhn check), phone numbers (int'l formats), SSN/CPF patterns, API keys. Add tests for each pattern.
Implement data retention policy: expire analytics events older than 30 days. Add manual purge endpoint for GDPR/compliance.
Replace detailed error messages with generic ones ('Conversion failed'). Log actual details only server-side with full context.
Review and harden Service Worker cache strategies: add explicit revalidate-on-use for /api/user/* endpoints and set Cache-Control: no-store for sensitive data.
Add process.on('exit') hook to github-safe.js to guarantee temp file cleanup even on abnormal termination.
Minimize User-Agent tracking: replace with minimal format or remove entirely since it's already available server-side from request headers.
Add IndexedDB quota monitoring: warn when >70%, clear old events when >90%, implement sliding window (max 30 days).
Run OWASP Dependency Check on package.json to identify vulnerable transitive dependencies.
Add CSP header to prevent inline scripts: script-src 'self' 'nonce-{random}' (no 'unsafe-inline').
Verify backend code (not shown) for: parameterized queries (no SQL injection), rate limiting on analytics endpoints, input validation on all user-controlled fields.
